reasons: Franco can punish Yve's stationary Real World Manipulation window with suppression.
Hook pressure and Bloody Hunt make it harder for Yve to cast safely from the backline.

proof.summary: Yve wants to stay positioned and continue Real World Manipulation, while Franco can suppress her and create a burst window before she controls the full fight.
worksBestWhen: Franco saves Bloody Hunt for Yve's real ultimate window.
Franco approaches from fog or Flicker range.
failureCases: Franco misses hook or cannot reach Yve.
Yve is protected by strong frontline peel.
Franco suppresses another target before Yve commits.

reasons: Kaja can suppress and drag Yve out of her preferred backline casting position.
Divine Judgment is a reliable answer when Yve commits to a high-value teamfight zone.

proof.summary: Yve wants uninterrupted area control, while Kaja can suppress and drag her into team follow-up.
worksBestWhen: Kaja has Flicker or flank access.
Kaja's team can immediately burst Yve after the pull.
failureCases: Kaja cannot reach through Yve's frontline.
Yve baits Kaja's engage before casting ultimate.
Kaja pulls Yve without enough damage follow-up.

reasons: Kadita can punish Yve when she stays in place during Real World Manipulation.
Kadita's mobility, airborne setup, and burst can reach Yve from side angles before Yve finishes controlling the fight.

proof.summary: Yve's ultimate asks her to commit to a fixed casting position, while Kadita can approach, knock her up, and burst during that commitment window.
worksBestWhen: Kadita enters from fog or a side angle.
Yve has already committed to ultimate positioning.
failureCases: Kadita misses Breath of the Ocean.
Yve stays too far behind frontline.
Kadita dives before Yve commits and gets controlled first.

reasons: Harley can enter quickly, mark Yve, burst her, and leave before her team fully responds.
His in-out burst is especially useful when Yve is casting or standing away from direct peel.

proof.summary: Yve wants long-range control from a fixed zone, while Harley can use Space Escape and Deadly Magic to punish her before she safely controls the fight.
worksBestWhen: Yve's defensive spell or peel is unavailable.
failureCases: Harley's burst is absorbed or cleansed by support timing.

reasons: Hayabusa can bypass normal front-to-back spacing and pressure Yve's fixed casting position.
His shadow mobility and assassination pattern punish Yve when she is isolated or lightly protected.

proof.summary: Yve wants safe backline space, while Hayabusa can bypass normal spacing and punish her with shadow mobility and single-target burst.
worksBestWhen: Hayabusa finds Yve isolated or lightly protected.
He waits for key enemy control skills to be used.
failureCases: Yve stays grouped with strong peel.
Hayabusa's damage spreads to other nearby units.
Hayabusa enters without enough cooldown or shadow setup.
